author: dhsorens_26042
content:
hi all, a brief async update on evm-asm:

**Headline**: Making strong progress on RV64/EVM opcode substrate; the stack-only EVM operations are done, but byte-addressed EVM memory is not yet fully integrated. Full zkEVM STF (including gas accounting) is still ahead.

**Done**:
- Verified RISC-V foundation: the project has a Lean model of the RV64 target, plus proof infrastructure for showing that generated RISC-V routines do what their specs say.
- RISC-V instruction confidence: the main RV64 instructions used by the EVM routines have been checked against the Sail RISC-V model.
- Core EVM opcode proofs: many stack-based opcodes are implemented and proved, including arithmetic, bitwise ops, comparisons, shifts, `BYTE`, `SIGNEXTEND`, stack manipulation, `PUSH0`, `MUL`, `DIV`, and `MOD`.
- EVM memory opcode progress: `MLOAD`, `MSTORE`, `MSTORE8`, and `MSIZE` now have proof surfaces, though memory integration is less polished than the stack-only opcodes.

**In Progress**:
- Bytecode and interpreter groundwork: the repo has foundations for reading EVM bytecode, decoding/dispatching opcodes, wiring opcode handlers, and proving fuel-bounded interpreter-step simulations.
- Execution-layer groundwork: RLP decoding, world-state/account models, transaction/block scaffolding, storage access/gas models, and precompile/host-call interfaces have started to land.

**Coming Next:**
- Finish remaining arithmetic opcode stack specs: `EXP`, `SDIV`, `SMOD`, `ADDMOD`, and `MULMOD`.
- Complete handler/interpreter integration so verified opcode subroutines become verified EVM execution steps.
- Fill out stateful EVM behavior: storage syscalls, calls/creates, logs, terminating opcodes, returndata, precompiles, and dynamic gas.
- Continue RLP/input pipeline work toward transaction and block ingestion.
- Prove transaction processing and block-level state transition, ending with a verified STF that writes the post-state root.

author: jsign
content:
New release of `projects/zkevm` specs and tests: [zkevm@v0.4.0](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-spec-tests/releases/tag/zkevm%40v0.4.0)

Quick summary:
- Based on `bal-devnet-7` (on top of [bal@v7.1.0 release](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-spec-tests/releases/tag/bal%40v7.1.0)).
- Testing framework:
    - New `pytest` marker for test authors to explicitly skip stateless runs.
   - Automatic skipping for fixtures that use raw-RLP-modifiers.
- Specs:
    - Add `schema-id` prefix in stateless guest input bytes to allow the guest program to properly interpret the rest of the input bytes.
   - Modify `ChainConfig` type to provide enough configuration tuning to allow the same guest program to work for EEST fixtures custom forks, mainnet, devnets, etc.
   - Properlly fill the `public_keys` field in `StatelessInput` which allows for more efficient verification of transaction signatures.

More details about all points in the [release description](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-spec-tests/releases/tag/zkevm%40v0.4.0).
